info.title: mt-commerce API
info.description: Open-source headless commerce platform for Indonesia. This document describes the public HTTP surface; routes will be fleshed out as each module ships.

message.description: Human-readable error message; safe to show to end users.

details.description: Optional, code-specific structured detail. For `validation_error` this contains an `issues` array.

plaintext.description: The freshly issued API key. Returned ONCE here. The database does not retain it.

amount.description: Decimal-string integer amount in the smallest unit of the currency.

total.description: Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.
Total matching items across all pages.

get.description: Returns 200 with `{ status: "ok" }` whenever the API process is up. Does not consult any dependencies.
Returns 200 when every checked dependency is reachable (currently just Postgres). Returns 503 otherwise.

503.description: Not ready — at least one dependency is unreachable.

delete.summary: Revoke one of the caller's sessions
delete.description: A staff user can only revoke their own sessions through this route. Cross-user revocation belongs to a separate owner-only endpoint and is not part of v0.1.

403.description: Cannot revoke a session that is not yours.

get.description: Returns every staff_profile row joined with the linked auth user's email. Used by the operator-facing staff & roles screen. Owner-only because the list reveals every operator's email and role.

post.summary: Create or update a staff profile (owner-only)

get.summary: List the caller's API keys (owner/admin only)

post.summary: Issue a new API key (owner/admin only)
post.description: The plaintext is returned ONCE in the response and not retained anywhere server-side. Callers must store it immediately.

get.description: Returns `{ user: null }` for anonymous callers, `{ user: { ... } }` when an authenticated session is present. Used by the storefront layout to render account state.
Paginated product list. Supports `categoryId`, `search`, price bounds, and sort. Translatable fields resolve from the locale chain (?locale, Accept-Language, default).

post.description: Create a product with a `translations` blob (see ADR-0010). Default locale must be present.

get.description: Flat list with `parentId`. Clients build a tree client-side.

post.description: Apply a signed `delta` to the variant's available count. Bounded to ±1,000,000 to keep the value safely inside int4. The optional `reason` is persisted to the audit log alongside the actor and the before/after counts.

get.summary: Get the inventory level for a variant
get.description: Returns the variant's single inventory row (location_id NULL in v0.1). 404 when the variant has no level row yet — every variant gets one on creation, so a 404 here means the variant id itself does not exist.

404.description: No inventory row for this variant.

get.description: Paginated list of inventory rows. `productId` narrows to one product's variants; without it, every variant is returned. Soft-deleted variants are excluded.

get.summary: List inventory audit history for a variant
get.description: Paginated audit_log rows where entity_kind=`inventory` and entity_id matches the variant id. Newest first. Each row carries the actor, the structured details (`deltaApplied`, `before`, `after`), the operator's `reason`, and the timestamp.
Returns active, non-deleted products. Supports `categorySlug`, `search`, price bounds, and sort. The `status` query field is ignored — drafts and archived items are never visible on the storefront.
Flat list with `parentId` so clients can build a tree client-side.
Paginated customer list. `email` and `search` narrow the result set; soft-deleted customers are excluded.

post.summary: Create an address for a customer

get.description: Returns the authenticated customer's profile. Until customer-auth lands the resolver reads the `x-customer-id` header.

patch.summary: Update one of the current customer's addresses

delete.summary: Delete one of the current customer's addresses

put.summary: Mark an address as default for a kind

get.summary: Look up subdistricts by postal code (public)
get.description: Multiple kelurahans can share a postal code; the response is a list.
Paginated cart list. Supports filtering by `status` and `customerId`. Each entry includes precomputed totals.

post.description: Force-transitions an active cart to `abandoned`. Refused for converted carts.

409.description: Cart cannot be abandoned in its current state.

post.description: Currency is locked at create-time and may not change later.

get.summary: Get a cart by id (public; ULID is the bearer)

post.description: Adding the same variant twice merges into a single line; the latest add's price wins.

409.description: Currency mismatch or cart not active.

post.summary: Remove all items from a cart

409.description: Cart is converted and cannot be cleared.

get.summary: Get the current customer's active cart

post.summary: Create or reuse the current customer's active cart
post.description: If the customer already has an active cart, returns it with status 200. Otherwise creates a new one and returns 201. Currency comes from the request body; the storefront passes its locale's currency.

get.description: Paginated list. Filter by `state` and `customerId`.

get.summary: List state-transition events for a checkout
get.description: Audit trail of checkout state transitions. Returns 404 if the checkout id does not exist (rather than an empty list, which could mask a typo).

post.summary: Start a checkout from a cart

409.description: Cart is not in a startable state.

put.summary: Set the shipping (and optional billing) address

post.description: Idempotent: requires an `Idempotency-Key` header. Replays return the same response without re-running the underlying transition.

409.description: Invalid state transition or idempotency conflict.

post.description: Optional body: `{ reason?: string | null }`. Cancellation is allowed from any non-terminal state and yields `state=failed`.

get.description: v0.1 expects exactly one fulfillment per order; the list shape leaves room for split shipments later.

patch.summary: Set or clear the tracking code
patch.description: Updates only the tracking code; does not change status. Pass `trackingCode: null` to clear.

409.description: Cannot set tracking on a cancelled fulfillment.

post.description: Transitions `pending → shipped` and stamps `tracked_at`. Optionally accepts a tracking code in the same request.

Transitions `shipped → delivered`, stamps `delivered_at`, and best-effort transitions the parent order `paid → fulfilled`. The order-side transition is silently skipped if the order is no longer in `paid` (e.g. it was cancelled or refunded between hand-off and delivery).
Transitions to `cancelled` from `pending` or `shipped`. Captures an optional reason on the audit row. Does NOT cancel the parent order.

get.description: Paginated list of every send attempt. Filter by `channel`, `kind`, and/or `status`.
Paginated list. Filter by status, customerId, email, an exact orderNumber (e.g. `ORD-2026-000123`), and a creation date range.

get.summary: Get an order by id, including status history
List status-history events for an order
get.description: Returns the audit trail. 404 when the order does not exist (rather than an empty list, which could mask a typo).

post.summary: Transition an order to a new status
post.description: Validates against the order state machine. Allowed transitions are documented in the orders module README.
Convenience wrapper around the `cancelled` transition that captures a free-text reason.

get.description: Returns the current customer's orders, newest first. Identifies the caller via the `x-customer-id` header until customer-auth integration lands.

200.description: Page of the current customer's orders.

get.description: Looks up by the human-readable `order_number` (friendlier for shareable URLs). Returns 404 if the order does not exist OR does not belong to the calling customer (no existence-leak across customers).

200.description: Order owned by the current customer.

get.description: Paginated list. Filter by orderId, status, and provider code.

get.summary: Get a payment with its attempt history

post.description: Idempotent: requires an `Idempotency-Key` header. Replays return the same response.
Idempotent: requires an `Idempotency-Key` header. Body accepts an optional `amount` (partial refund) and `reason`.

post.summary: Initiate payment for a completed checkout
post.description: Requires an `Idempotency-Key` header — the same key dedupes the underlying `payments` row, so a retry returns the same outcome instead of charging twice.

200.description: Payment outcome — redirect, captured, or pending.

404.description: Checkout has no associated order yet.

409.description: Order is not in a payable state, or provider is unknown.

get.summary: Get the payment for this checkout
get.description: Returns the payment row attached to the order this checkout produced. 404 when no payment has been initiated yet.
Returns the singleton settings row. The first call lazily inserts the default row, so callers never receive a `not_found` for this endpoint.

patch.description: Partial update. Send only the keys you want to change. Pass `null` to clear an optional field (e.g. `defaultTaxRateId: null`).

post.description: Generic webhook endpoint. Dispatches to the provider's `verifyWebhookSignature`. Returns `{ status: 'accepted' | 'ignored' }` — `ignored` covers unknown payment refs and idempotent re-deliveries.

400.description: Signature verification failed or unknown provider.
